text: The LORD Speaks of His Creation

segments.text: Do you know when mountain goats give birth? Have you watched the doe bear her fawn?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you count the months they are pregnant? Do you know the time they give birth?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They crouch down and bring forth their young; they deliver their newborn.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Their young ones thrive and grow up in the open field; they leave and do not return.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who set the wild donkey free? Who released the swift donkey from the harness?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I made the wilderness his home and the salt flats his dwelling.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He scorns the tumult of the city and never hears the shouts of a driver.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He roams the mountains for pasture, searching for any green thing.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Will the wild ox consent to serve you? Will he stay by your manger at night?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you hold him to the furrow with a harness? Will he plow the valleys behind you?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you rely on his great strength? Will you leave your hard work to him?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you trust him to bring in your grain and gather it to your threshing floor?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The wings of the ostrich flap joyfully, but cannot match the pinions and feathers of the stork.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For she leaves her eggs on the ground and lets them warm in the sand.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: She forgets that a foot may crush them, or a wild animal may trample them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: She treats her young harshly, as if not her own, with no concern that her labor was in vain.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For God has deprived her of wisdom; He has not endowed her with understanding.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Yet when she proudly spreads her wings, she laughs at the horse and its rider.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do you give strength to the horseor adorn his neck with a mane?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do you make him leap like a locust, striking terror with his proud snorting?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He paws in the valley and rejoices in his strength; he charges into battle.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He laughs at fear, frightened of nothing; he does not turn back from the sword.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: A quiver rattles at his side, along with a flashing spear and lance.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Trembling with excitement, he devours the distance; he cannot st and still when the ram’s horn sounds.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
At the blast of the horn, he snorts with fervor. He catches the scent of battle from afar—
the shouts of captains and the cry of war.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Does the hawk take flight by your understanding and spread his wings toward the south?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Does the eagle soar at your command and make his nest on high?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He dwells on a cliff and lodges there; his stronghold is on a rocky crag.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: From there he spies out food; his eyes see it from afar.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His young ones feast on blood; and where the slain are, there he is.
segments.speaker: The LORD
